rules.name: Lint leakage
rules.summary: Style rules a linter or formatter already enforces. Found in 62% of mined AGENTS.md files; never send an LLM to do a linter's job.
rules.recommendation: Delete the rule and point to the linter instead, e.g. "run `eslint --fix`; the linter config is the source of truth."

rules.name: Directory tree / codebase tour
rules.summary: Repo maps and directory listings increased agent exploration without improving file discovery in the ETH ablation. The agent can run `ls`.
rules.recommendation: Delete the tree. Keep at most one line per genuinely non-obvious location ("API handlers live in src/api/handlers/").

rules.name: Vague / aspirational rule
rules.summary: Anthropic's exclude list names self-evident practices like "write clean code". Vague words ("appropriately", "best practices") give the agent nothing testable.
rules.recommendation: Replace with a concrete, verifiable instruction (exact command, path, or example) or delete the line.

rules.name: No runnable commands
rules.summary: Exact build/test/lint commands are the one content class proven to change agent behavior (tools named in the file get used 1.6x as often). This file has none.
rules.recommendation: Add a commands section first: exact build, test, and lint invocations with flags, in backticks.

rules.name: Hard rule as advisory prose
rules.summary: "Unlike CLAUDE.md instructions which are advisory, hooks are deterministic" (Anthropic). NEVER/ALWAYS rules on mechanically-enforceable behaviors are requests, not laws.
rules.recommendation: Keep the line if you accept ~advisory compliance; for zero-exception rules add a hook, lint rule, or CI check.

rules.name: Possible secret
rules.summary: A token-shaped string in an instruction file ships to every agent session and usually to git.
rules.recommendation: Remove the credential, rotate it, and reference an env var name instead.

rules.name: Emphasis overuse
rules.summary: IMPORTANT/YOU MUST works because it is rare. Past a handful of uses the emphasis dilutes and nothing stands out.
rules.recommendation: Keep emphasis on the 1-3 rules that genuinely matter most; demote the rest to plain statements.

rules.name: Doc pointers without a read instruction
rules.summary: Progressive-disclosure link lists get ignored unless a load-bearing instruction tells the agent to read them before starting.
rules.recommendation: Prefix the list with an explicit imperative, e.g. "IMPORTANT: read the relevant doc below before starting work."

rules.name: Length past 200 lines
rules.summary: Anthropic's official target is under 200 lines. Honest caveat: a 1,650-session factorial study (arXiv 2605.10039) found no compliance effect from file size — the argument for brevity is token cost (+14-23% per session in the ETH study), not adherence.
rules.recommendation: Cut code-derivable content first (trees, stack restatements, style rules); push sometimes-relevant knowledge into on-demand docs or skills.

rules.name: Duplicate rule
rules.summary: Near-identical instructions accumulate as files grow by accretion; each duplicate spends budget without adding signal.
rules.recommendation: Keep one occurrence, delete the rest.

rules.name: Contradictory instructions
rules.summary: Contradictory rules get resolved arbitrarily (Anthropic docs) — the agent follows one and ignores the other, inconsistently.
rules.recommendation: Pick one convention, delete the other, and let a linter or lockfile enforce it where possible.

rules.name: No boundaries declared
rules.summary: GitHub's 2,500-repo analysis found explicit boundaries (always do / ask first / never do) the strongest single pattern; "never commit secrets" was the most commonly helpful constraint.
rules.recommendation: Add a short boundaries section: what the agent must never do, and what needs asking first.

rules.name: Exceeds Codex 32 KiB cap
rules.summary: OpenAI Codex stops reading at project_doc_max_bytes (32 KiB default) — everything past the cap is silently truncated.
rules.recommendation: Cut below 32 KiB, or split into nested per-directory files.

rules.name: Dead command
rules.summary: The file references a script that does not exist in package.json. Agents follow file-named tools 1.6x as often — a dead command sends every session down a broken path.
rules.recommendation: Fix the command to match package.json scripts, or delete it.

rules.name: Stale path
rules.summary: The file references a path that does not exist in the repo. Stale content is the top drift failure mode as code moves and the file doesn't.
rules.recommendation: Update the path or delete the reference.

rules.name: Duplicates README
rules.summary: The ETH ablation's key finding: context files help only when non-redundant with existing docs (+2.7% with docs stripped, ~0 with them present). This line restates the README the agent already reads.
rules.recommendation: Delete the line; keep only what the README and code cannot tell the agent.

rules.name: Invisible to Claude Code
rules.summary: Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md (feature request #6235 is open since Aug 2025). This repo has AGENTS.md with no CLAUDE.md bridge, so the most-used agent never sees it.
rules.recommendation: Add a one-line CLAUDE.md containing `@AGENTS.md`. A symlink also works for reading; the import is slightly safer because Claude Code refuses to WRITE through symlinks (issue #66559), which only matters if you let it update the file.

rules.name: Unresolved @-import
rules.summary: A CLAUDE.md @-import points at a file that does not exist in the repo, so the import silently loads nothing.
rules.recommendation: Fix the import path or remove the import.
